We are given the 4×4 matrix:

$$
A = \begin{pmatrix}
\frac{4}{3} & 1 & -\frac{9}{2} & -\frac{13}{3} \\
-\frac{13}{3} & \frac{25}{6} & -\frac{19}{6} & \frac{11}{6} \\
-\frac{23}{6} & \frac{29}{6} & -\frac{10}{3} & -\frac{19}{6} \\
-\frac{17}{6} & -\frac{7}{6} & \frac{5}{2} & \frac{7}{3}
\end{pmatrix}
$$

We are to find the **determinant** of this matrix.

---

### Step 1: Use cofactor expansion

We can use cofactor expansion along the first row for simplicity.

$$
\det(A) = \frac{4}{3} \cdot \det\left(\begin{pmatrix}
\frac{25}{6} & -\frac{19}{6} & \frac{11}{6} \\
\frac{29}{6} & -\frac{10}{3} & -\frac{19}{6} \\
-\frac{7}{6} & \frac{5}{2} & \frac{7}{3}
\end{pmatrix}\right)

-\frac{13}{3} & -\frac{19}{6} & \frac{11}{6} \\
-\frac{23}{6} & -\frac{10}{3} & -\frac{19}{6} \\
-\frac{17}{6} & \frac{5}{2} & \frac{7}{3}
\end{pmatrix}\right)
